relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Mercy-given ministry keeps the heart from losing courage.
Hidden shame and craftiness are renounced for manifest truth.
If the good news is veiled, the veil belongs to perishing perception.
The age’s false god blinds thoughts from Messiah’s glory-light.
Paul proclaims not self but Jesus as Lord and himself as servant.
The Creator’s first light now shines in hearts through Jesus’ face.
Treasure rests in clay vessels so power is known as Divine.
Pressed but not crushed; perplexed but not despairing.
Pursued but not abandoned; struck down but not destroyed.
The body carries Jesus’ dying so Jesus’ life may manifest in the body.
Mortal flesh becomes a place where Jesus’ life is revealed.
Death works in the servant so life can work in the community.
Trust speaks because it has trusted.
The One who raised Jesus will raise and present us together.
Grace multiplied through many becomes thanksgiving into Divine glory.
Outer decay does not cancel daily inner renewal.
Momentary pressure works an immeasurable weight of glory.
The seen is temporary; the unseen is age-long.

contemplative_note: 2 Corinthians 4 is treasure-in-clay medicine. Vulnerability is not failure; clay becomes the vessel where the light of Jesus’ face and the power of resurrection are seen.
